insight: Thorium Dynamo is argued to be too strong due to three factors: it centralizes breachproof green strategies more than any other unit, it homogenizes opening build orders (P1 almost always plays DD/D1, P2 plays DD/11/D), and it has very little opportunity cost compared to other alt drones like Vivid Drone, Wild Drone, or Doomed Drone, which each have meaningful weaknesses.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 laid out a multi-point case for nerfing Thorium Dynamo, with namington partially agreeing that openings are set in stone but noting mid/late game diversity remains.

insight: Vivid Drone is more breach-vulnerable than a regular Drone and can be targeted by Apollo; Wild Drone can be sniped in sets where absorbing is important; Doomed Drone is slightly less efficient than a regular Drone for full economy; Thorium Dynamo avoids all these downsides, making it almost always correct to buy in any set it appears.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion comparing alternative drones and their respective weaknesses versus Thorium Dynamo's near-absence of downsides.

insight: In Centrifuge sets, P1 almost always has the better opening line. Specific examples: Lucina sets favor DD/DD/DD1, Vai Mauronax sets favor DD/D1, Amporilla (Bloodrager) sets favor DD/DD/1A, and Wincer sets favor opening with 1. P2 can find better lines mainly when alternative drones are present.
author: masn6811
context: masn6811 discussing Centrifuge balance and listing specific opening lines across various named unit sets.

insight: Tantalum Ray's nerf (removing one Gauss Charge click, reducing its damage output) dropped it from an autopurchase/top-tier unit to a more average one, estimated from roughly the 85th percentile of unit power to the 55th-60th percentile. The nerf's impact is somewhat mitigated by economic inflation over the course of a game.
author: consensus
context: Discussion triggered by a viewer watching Golden Cup VODs and asking about pre-nerf Tantalum Ray's power level.

insight: Old Drake (14BBB for 3 damage, 5 health) was considered heavily player-favored and required four successive 1-gold nerfs before being reworked to its current form, reflecting that early Prismata development consistently undervalued the worth of pressure units.
author: apooche
context: Community members discussing historically overpowered unit versions; apooche and iminabearsuit confirmed details about old Drake's stats and nerf history.

insight: Old Scorchilla (Scorch) with no build time was considered the most player-favored unit ever in Prismata history. The double-Scorch opening for P2 was particularly dominant, and P1's attempt to go half a turn faster was simply not viable against it.
author: apooche
context: Retrospective discussion of historically broken units; apooche called old Scorch the biggest player-advantage unit ever, with iminabearsuit confirming the double-Scorch P2 opening.

insight: Old Cryo Ray had no lifespan and functioned like a Shiver Yeti for the same cost, making it arguably the most overpowered historically changed unit. Old Centurion cost approximately 16GBR, and old Antima Comet had 4 supply, did not require red mana, and was still considered very powerful despite requiring precise timing.
author: iminabearsuit
context: iminabearsuit sharing screenshots and recollections of historically overpowered unit versions, with community members reacting to the old stats.

insight: Old Centrifuge was considered extremely powerful (pre-nerf), generating 12GGBBRR on sacrifice at a cost of 9 gold. It was nerfed to cost 5 gold and generate only 3GGBBRR. Some community members feel the current version is too weak, particularly because P1 usually has the better line in Centrifuge sets.
author: consensus
context: Community members discussing Centrifuge's history from broken to potentially over-nerfed, with specific pre- and post-nerf stat figures cited.

insight: In Venge Cannon all-in sets, players often float large amounts of gold because spending it on Drones or Engineers is suboptimal during the all-in. Auric Impulse is particularly valuable in these situations if you already have an Engineer, providing a gold sink with meaningful upside.
author: xyotsuba
context: Discussion following amalloy's claim that failing to buy any Auric Impulse by game's end is a mistake, with xyotsuba giving the Venge all-in as a specific use case.

insight: amalloy asserts that reaching the end of an Auric Impulse game without having bought a single one is a mistake, implying the unit is almost always worth purchasing at some point in games where it appears.
author: amalloy
context: amalloy responding to totally_not_fbi's claim of having a high win rate with Auric Impulse by buying almost none of them.

insight: Master Bot, when placed in ranked play, achieved Tier 6 with 48% progression toward Tier 7, according to the published AAAI paper on its AI. Community members note this means MB has not crossed the 'bell curve hump' of the ranked player distribution and would not achieve a high rank.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion about what rank Master Bot would achieve if placed in ranked queue; amalloy cited the official AAAI paper with specific tier data.

insight: Master Bot's ranked performance peaked at Tier 6 (48% toward Tier 7), indicating it plays at approximately a Tier 5-6 human level. It has not overcome the difficulty peak of the ranked player distribution, making it consistently beatable by mid-to-high tier ranked players.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 and amalloy discussing Master Bot's historical ranked performance, with amalloy citing the AAAI paper and mrguy888 noting MB has not gotten over the bell curve.

insight: Savior is considered strategically binary: the first player to build one at the 'right' moment wins, but mrguy888 disputes the simpler 'first Savior wins' framing, arguing P2 has two approximately equivalent Savior timings — one half-turn before P1's reasonable timing (better in high-pressure sets for extra plasma) and one half-turn after (better in high-soak sets where gold matters more and early plasma is weaker).
author: mrguy888
context: iminabearsuit complained Savior is binary and the first reasonable one always wins; mrguy888 corrected this with a more nuanced view of P2 Savior timing windows.

insight: Random set drones (alternate drones from the card pool) do not get doubled by Savior, so they are treated separately when evaluating Savior timing and drone sacrifice decisions.
author: mrguy888
context: Discussion of how alternative drones interact with Savior's doubling mechanic during the Savior timing conversation.

insight: Deadeye Operative is best countered in fast-paced sets by building attackers and killing the Deadeye player before the Drone-sniping becomes decisive; in slow games, the Deadeye threat is less pressing and does not necessarily require an immediate counter-response.
author: amalloy
context: willflame expressed confusion about playing around Deadeye Operative; amalloy provided the conditional strategic response based on game pace.
